## Photo Specifications
Kindly Note that PHOTOBOOTH is available at VFS Application Center and Photos provided are as per the OACI specifications

## Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must be:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers

- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have
                                                                            them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

The photographs must be:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 days. This period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen area and back again during your visit.
Nationals of these countries should submit the application with a minimum advance indicated - 15 days from the date of trip since they are subject to prior consultation of the central authorities..

                                                                        To get an update on the status of your application: 

                                                                        You may visit our website https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html)                                                                        and click on the link “Track your Application”. Please keep your date of birth and receipt number information handy while tracking your application status.

                                                                        Or Email us at: info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com)
Or Contact our Helpline at: +258 846281607 
Also one may request for an SMS update service which is an automated confirmation feedback message sent to the applicant's mobile phone, at various stages of the visa application process.

It Is Not Permitted Double Nationals (Portuguese Or Eu / Mozambican) To Apply For A Schengen Visa
Applications can be applied not more than six months before the start of the intended trip. It is advisable to apply an application at least 15 calendar days before the intended visit.
                                                                        
List of supporting documents to be presented by visa applicants in Mozambique
- General requirements for all visa applicants
- Physical presence of the applicant;
- Complete the Schengen visa application form: electronically (available at http://www.vfsglobal.com/portugal/mozambique/pdf/Visit-Schengen-Eng.pdf)
)
- Signature of the Schengen form:
- Adult: by own;
- Minors: consent of parental authority or legal guardian must be required only if the minor travels alone or with only one parent. Exceptions must be made in the case of single mother with whom the minor should travel has only parental authority. Consent should be given in the legal form applied in Mozambique.
- Passport:
- Issued less than 10 years, with validity of (at least) 3 months following the expected date of departure from the Schengen territory
- With (atleast) 3 blank pages;
- Valid for more than 3 months (from the date of departure from the Schengen area)
- Photocopy of the Passport (identification sheet and previous Schengen visas)
- Non-Mozambicans: Foreign Resident Identification Card –
DIRE
.
- A photograph in color, current - do not glue or staple;
- The return-ticket reservation (the issued ticket must be shown when the visa is issued)
- Travel insurance: Covering at least € 30,000, including the cost that will have to be paid in case you have to return to your country of origin due to illness or death. This insurance is valid for all Schengen area and for whole period of your first stay.
- MINORS
- Photocopy of the identification document of the child and the parents;
- Authorization of the parent who does not travel with the child, or both, if traveling with third parties, recognized in the Mozambican notary (form available at:
 
http://www.vfsglobal.com/portugal/mozambique/pdf/menores.pdf
) or the document of the court if the child is only under the custody of a guardian;
- If one of the parents is deceased: Certificate of death;
- If you are a student: Declaration from the School / University you attend.
- If the minor travels with the parents, they must present the flight reservations.
- EU CITIZENS 'FAMILY, FAMILY CITIZENS - traveling to and from the Schengen area accompanied by the EU / EEE citizen
- Photocopy of the marriage certificate: when done at a Portuguese civil registry office and / or proof of relationship (Declaration by local Authority).
- Identification document of the EU / EEE relative.
- Declaration of the Portuguese spouse to the spouse of different nationality (since traveling with the spouse or proving that he/she is going to meet their partner).

Applies to the following citizens:
- The spouse;
- The partner with whom a citizen of the union has entered into a registered partnership based on the legislation of a Member State (de facto unions), if the law of the host Member State considers registered partnerships to be equivalent to marriage;
- Direct descendants under the age of 21 or dependent, as well as those of the spouse or partner as defined above, other direct ascendants who are in charge as the spouse of their partner.
- Note: Photocopy of travel medical insurance is not necessary for the Schengen area.
- Exempted visa: Exempted visa is valid only when it is proven that the applicant meets or travels with the European citizen (flight reservation with European citizen.)
- Documents to be presented depending on the purpose of travel
- Travelling for the purpose of official mission or official purposes
- For Mozambican citizens: Declaration from the Ministry or the Institution concerned
- For non-Mozambicans: Declaration from the embassy of the country concerned and the diplomatic card
- Travelling for the purpose of business-conference (professional purposes)
- Letter of invitation from the company or conference institution to be visited
- Letter from the appropriate body (e.g. employer) explaining the purpose of the trip
- Letter from the party covering the travel costs and the stay
- Travelling for the purpose of family visit
- Term of Responsibility (available at: http://www.vfsglobal.com/portugal/mozambique/pdf/modelo-termo.pdf) signed by the person who invites and recognized in the Portugal notarized.
- Copy of identity card / passport / Authorization of residence of the inviting person.
- Proof of residence in Portugal: Attestation of residence from the Junta de Freguesia / 3 last receipts of the payment of water, electricity or gas.
- Three last paycheck receipts or IRS;
- Proof of family ties (e.g. birth certificate or marriage certificate) with the host or inviting person
- Employers' declaration that mentions: name, date of birth, position they have held since they joined the company, monthly salary, since when they work at the current company, and are allowed for vacations;
(Austria, Denmark [1], Germany, Spain, France, Italy, Luxembourg, Norway, the Netherlands and Portugal requires applicants to present such proof by means of a national form (cf. the websites of the Member State concerned)
- Travelling for the purpose of tourism
- Present proof of itinerary, particularly if the person concerned is to visit more than one Member State
- Hotel reservations
- Bank account statement in the applicant's name which must show movement over a certain period of time, e.g. the last three months
- Letter of Employer (proof of work contract, certificate of employment, information on professional status)
- Travelling for the purpose of studies, training or internship
- Proof of enrolment at the institution concerned
- Bank account statement in the applicant's name which must show movement over a certain period of time, e.g. the last three months
- Travelling for the purpose of medical treatment
- Certificate from a medical doctor and/or medical institution that the claimed necessary treatment is not available in Mozambique
- An official document of the receiving medical institution confirming that it can perform the specific medical treatment and the patient will be accepted accordingly
- Proof of sufficient financial means to pay for the medical treatment and related expenses (bank account statements in the applicant's name which must show movement over a certain period of time, e.g. the last three months)
- Proof of pre-payment of treatment.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to visit Portugal or a member state (Lithuania, Czech Republic, Greece, Slovakia, Estonia, Norway) for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you
                                                                        come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Schengen.
The issue of Schengen visas is discretionary and depends on examination of each individual case, including purpose of visit and intention to return. The short-stay visa does not automatically
                                                                        entitle you to enter the Schengen area. At border (or during other controls) you may have to show the visa but also provide additional documentation, for example information on that
                                                                        you have sufficient means to cover the stay and the return trip. It is therefore recommended that you carry with you copies of the documents which you presented when applying for
                                                                        the visa (e.g. letter of invitation, travel confirmations, other documents stating the purpose of your stay).

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/prt/travel-insurance)
DO NOT ALLOW TWO NATIONALS (PORTUGUESE / MOZAMBICAN) OR NATIONALITY HOLDERS OF ONE OF THE SCHENGEN STATES; TO SUBMIT A NATIONAL VISA APPLICATION
TO APPLY THE VISA APPLICATION MUST BE MADE WITH AT LEAST 30 WORKING DAYS BEFORE THE PLAN DATE FOR THE TRAVEL, IN THIS CASE OF THE VISAS OF TEMPORARY STAY, AND OF 60 DAYS FOR RESIDENCE
                                                                        VISA. THERE ARE EXCEPTIONS EXPECTED TO THESE TERMS IN ACCORDANCE WITH CURRENT LEGISLATION.
List of documents to be submitted by visa applicants in Maputo:
The job seeker visa entitles its holder to enter and remain only in Portugal for the purpose of looking for job; authorizes him/her to carry out a subordinated work activity, until the visa expires or until the residence permit is granted.
This visa is granted for a period of 120 days, renewable for another 60 days and allows only one entry into Portugal.
The issuance of this visa supposes the attribution of a date of scheduling in the competent services, within the period of validity of 120 days of the visa, and it allows the applicant, after the formalization of the employment contract throughout that period, the right to apply for a residence permit. To do so, you must fulfil the general conditions for granting a temporary residence permit, under the terms of article 77 of the Law.
	Once the maximum validity limit of the visa for looking for work has expired, without an employment relationship having been established nor the process of requesting the granting of a residence permit having begun, the visa holder must leave the country.
In these situations, you can only re-apply for a new visa application for this purpose, one year after the expiry of the previous visa's validity.
The request for a visa extension by a job seeker visa holder must be accompanied by proof of registration with the IEFP, I.P., and a declaration by the applicant indicating that the conditions of the planned stay are maintained, which will be assessed considering the reasons that justified its issuance.
Required DOCUMENTATION
- National Visa application (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/images/formulario_visto_nacional_en.pdf)
filled and duly signed by applicant;
- Passport or other travel document, valid for 3 months after the estimated date of return. Photocopy of passport (biographical data);
- 2 Photos, even, passport type, recent and in good condition to identify the applicant (1 for this form);
- Proof of regular situation in case of residence in a country other than the country of current nationality;
- Request for criminal record enquiry by the Immigration and Border Services (SEF) (Not applicable to minors under the age of sixteen);
- Criminal record certificate, issued by the competent authority of the country of the applicant’s nationality or of the country where the applicant has resided for over a year (except for applicants under sixteen), with the Hague Apostille (If applicable) or legalised;
- Valid travel insurance, covering necessary medical expenses, including urgent medical assistance and possible repatriation;
- Transport document - Roundtrip flight reservation showing date of departure and date of return;
- Proof of financial resources equivalent to at least the sum of three guaranteed monthly minimum salary.
Proof of the financial resources may be waived upon presentation of a term of responsibility with recognized signature made by a Portuguese citizen or a foreign citizen, with a legal residence authorization in Portugal, which guarantees food and accommodation for the visa applicant, as well as the repatriation costs, in case of irregular stay.
The signatory of the responsibility term must also prove to have financial capacity in the amount of, at least, three times the value of the guaranteed minimum monthly salary (€705).
Please note that at the time of submitting your application, you must present the original Term of Responsibility. Copies are not accepted.
SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS
- Declaration with indication of the conditions for the estimated stay.
- Proof of presentation of a declaration of expression of interest for enrolment in the IEFP (https://formularios.iefp.pt/index.php/929227?lang=en) (EN)/ (PT) (https://formularios.iefp.pt/index.php/929227?lang=pt) /(FR) (https://formularios.iefp.pt/index.php/929227?lang=en) /(ES) (https://formularios.iefp.pt/index.php/929227?lang=en).

## Photo Specifications
Kindly Note that PHOTOBOOTH is available at VFS Application Center and Photos provided are as per the OACI specifications

## Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must be:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers

- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have
                                                                            them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

The photographs must be:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
This Visa is for residency visa for accompanying family member applying for residence visa.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This Visa is for residency visa for accompanying family member applying for residence visa.

## Visa Fees
The visa fees applicable to the national visa application are as mention below table:

[Table:D- Residence visa for accompanying family member]
The applicants who wish to appeal their visa application have to pay a 329 MZN service fee and a 5553 (75 euros) visa fee.
Applicant who are exempt from Visa fees:
- Descendants of holders of Residence Permit under the provisions of family reunification.
- Foreign citizens who benefit from scholarships granted by the Portuguese State.
- To carry out highly qualified research activities (residence and temporary stay).
- Patients beneficiaries of Cooperation Agreements with Portugal in the field of health and
The fee is not refundable, refers only to the processing of the visa application and does not imply its issuance.
A service fee of 40 EUR (inclusive of 16% tax) will be added to the applicable visa fee for each application. This fee is payable only at the VFS Visa Application Center and must be paid in local currency, based on the current month’s exchange rate provided by the Consulate. Family members of EU/EEA or Swiss citizens are exempt from paying the service fee.
All the payments At Visa application centre are to be made in Cash,
We also offer an SMS Notification Service at a cost of 148 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a quick and convenient way to know the status of your visa application. This service is optional.
We also have to offer a Preferred VIP Lounge Service at a cost of 4442 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a comfortable way to apply for your visa and offers faster service to its users for submission process only.
We also have to offer Photocopy service at the cost of 15 MT (16 % tax included ).
We also have to offer Photograph service at the cost of 370 MT (16 % tax included ).
We also have to offer form filling service at the cost of 370 MT (16 % tax included ).
After the application has been submitted to the VFS Visa Application Center, there is no refund of the Service fees.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/prt/travel-insurance)
DO NOT ALLOW TWO NATIONALS (PORTUGUESE / MOZAMBICAN) OR NATIONALITY HOLDERS OF ONE OF THE SCHENGEN STATES; TO SUBMIT A NATIONAL VISA APPLICATION
TO APPLY THE VISA APPLICATION MUST BE MADE WITH AT LEAST 30 WORKING DAYS BEFORE THE PLAN DATE FOR THE TRAVEL, IN THIS CASE OF THE VISAS OF TEMPORARY STAY, AND OF 60 DAYS FOR RESIDENCE
                                                                        VISA. THERE ARE EXCEPTIONS EXPECTED TO THESE TERMS IN ACCORDANCE WITH CURRENT LEGISLATION.
List of documents to be submitted by visa applicants in Maputo:
Documents: 
- National Visa application (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/images/formulario_visto_nacional_en.pdf)
filled and duly signed by applicant (For minors and incapacitated, forms should be signed the legal guardian);
- 2 Photos, even, passport type, recent and in good condition to identify the applicant (1 for this form);
- Passport or other travel document, valid for 3 months after the estimated date of return. Photocopy of passport (biographical data);
- Proof of regular situation in case of residence in a country other than the country of current nationality, which must be valid 3 months after the estimated date of return;
- Valid travel insurance, covering necessary medical expenses, including urgent medical assistance and possible repatriation; (*)
- Criminal record certificate, issued by the competent authority of the country of the applicant’s nationality or of the country where the applicant has resided for over a year (except for applicants under sixteen), with the Hague Apostille (If applicable) or legalised;
- Request for criminal record enquiry (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/images/schengen/formularios/aut_consulta_rc.pdf)
by the Immigration and Border Services (SEF) (Not applicable to minors under the age of sixteen);
- Transport document - Roundtrip flight reservation showing date of departure and date of return (except for residence visas); (*)
- Proof of financial resources. (*)
The proof of means of subsistence can be made by submitting a term of responsibility signed by the host entity for interns or workers, as well as by the organization responsible for student exchange or volunteer programs.
(*) Please note that this requirement may be waived in the case of a Bilateral/International Agreement between Portugal and the country of origin concerning medical assistance that qualifies certain beneficiaries with recognized insurance, as for example in the case of Brazil (PB4) and the United Kingdom (S1).
For accompanying family member applying for a residence visa
- Proof of family bond that justify the accompaniment;
- Proof of availability of stable and regular means, enough to cover the needs of the residency visa applicant and the accompanying relatives during the required period of stay or for the period of 12 months, whatever is lesser, under the provisions laid out on the nº 2 of article 2 of Ordinance nº 1563/2007, of 11 December.
- Adult sons, dependent on the couple or on one spouse, single and that study in a teaching establishment in Portugal;
- Under aged sons, under the care of the couple, or of one spouse, that are single and study, if the holder of the right to family reunion is the bearer of a residence permit issued under the article 90.º-A;
- The ascendants in direct line and in first degree of the resident or of his spouse, if they are under their care;
- Under aged brothers, if they are dependent of the resident, as declared by a decision of the competent authority of the country of origin and that the decision be recognised in Portugal;
- The partner, in Portugal or in other country, with whom the foreign resident citizen has a civil union, duly proved under the law;
- Unmarried sons, under aged or incapable, including adopted sons of the partner in the civil union, if they are legally under the protection of the partner.
Note:

This type is not intended for family members of legal residents in Portugal.
Family members of foreign citizens legally residing in Portugal must apply for a Family Reunification visa, after the family reunification is granted to legal resident by Foreigners and Borders Service. (https://imigrante.sef.pt/solicitar/residir/art98-1/)
- Checklist D-Residence-Visa-for-accompanying-family-member.pdf

## Photo Specifications
Kindly Note that PHOTOBOOTH is available at VFS Application Center and Photos provided are as per the OACI specifications

## Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must be:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers

- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have
                                                                            them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

The photographs must be:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 days. This period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen area and back again during your visit.
Nationals of these countries should submit the application with a minimum advance indicated - 15 days from the date of trip since they are subject to prior consultation of the central authorities..

                                                                        To get an update on the status of your application: 

                                                                        You may visit our website https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html)                                                                        and click on the link “Track your Application”. Please keep your date of birth and receipt number information handy while tracking your application status.

                                                                        Or Email us at: info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com)
Or Contact our Helpline at: +258 846281607 
Also one may request for an SMS update service which is an automated confirmation feedback message sent to the applicant's mobile phone, at various stages of the visa application process.

 Visa application form must be completed for all the applicants including minors electronically filled on your computer and printed. The Visa Application Form has to be filled out in Portuguese
                                                                        language only. The form needs to be signed personally by the applicant in the field 37 and on the last page.
For minors, the application must be signed by both the parents or legal guardian.
The form below must be downloaded, electronically filled and printed to be displayed:

- Patients beneficiaries of Cooperation Agreements with Portugal in the field of health and
The fee is not refundable, refers only to the processing of the visa application and does not imply its issuance.
A service fee of 40 EUR (inclusive of 16% tax) will be added to the applicable visa fee for each application. This fee is payable only at the VFS Visa Application Center and must be paid in local currency, based on the current month’s exchange rate provided by the Consulate. Family members of EU/EEA or Swiss citizens are exempt from paying the service fee.
All the payments At Visa application centre are to be made in Cash,
We also offer an SMS Notification Service at a cost of 148 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a quick and convenient way to know the status of your visa application. This service is optional.
We also have to offer a Preferred VIP Lounge Service at a cost of 4442 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a comfortable way to apply for your visa and offers faster service to its users for submission process only.

filled and duly signed by applicant (For minors and incapacitated, forms should be signed the legal guardian);

- Valid travel insurance, covering necessary medical expenses, including urgent medical assistance and possible repatriation; (*)

by the Immigration and Border Services (SEF) (Not applicable to minors under the age of sixteen);

Checklist D-Residence-Visa-Digital-Nomads.pdf

## Photo Specifications
Kindly Note that PHOTOBOOTH is available at VFS Application Center and Photos provided are as per the OACI specifications

## Photograph Quality

The photographs must be:
- No more than 6-months old
- 35-40mm in width
- Close up of your head and top of your shoulders so that your face takes up 70 -80% of the photograph
- In sharp focus and clear
- Of high quality with no ink marks or creases

The photographs must be:
- Show you looking directly at the camera
- Show your skin tones naturally
- Have appropriate brightness and contrast
- Be printed on high quality paper, and at high resolution Photographs taken with a digital camera must be high quality colour and printed on photo-quality paper.

## Style and lighting

The photographs must be:
- Be colour neutral
- Show your eyes open and clearly visible-no hair across your eyes
- Show you facing square on to the camera, not looking over one shoulder (portrait style) or tilted, and showing both edges of your face clearly
- Be taken with a plain light-coloured background
- Be taken with uniform lighting and not show shadows or flash reflections on your face and no red eye

## Glasses and head covers

- If you wear glasses:
- Be colour neutral
- The photograph must show your eyes clearly with no flash reflection off the glasses, and no tinted lenses (if possible, avoid heavy frames - wear lighter framed glasses if you have
                                                                            them)
- Make sure that the frames do not cover any part of your eyes.
- Head coverings :
- Are not permitted except for religious reasons, but your facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of your face must be clearly shown.

## Expression and frame

The photographs must be:
- Show you alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible), looking at the camera with a neutral expression and your mouth closed.

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 days. This period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen area and back again during your visit.
Nationals of these countries should submit the application with a minimum advance indicated - 15 days from the date of trip since they are subject to prior consultation of the central authorities..

                                                                        You may visit our website https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html  (https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html)                                                                        and click on the link “Track your Application”. Please keep your date of birth and receipt number information handy while tracking your application status.

                                                                        Or Email us at: info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com)
Or Contact our Helpline at: +258 846281607 
Also one may request for an SMS update service which is an automated confirmation feedback message sent to the applicant's mobile phone, at various stages of the visa application process.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Long-stay visas entitle the holder to stay or establish residence in Portugal according to the duration of the stay and the reason for the stay.
They allow stays for periods of 4 months with two entries, in order to request the fixing of residence with the foreigners and border service. According to the reasons for the stay, there
                                                                        are 6 visa subtypes, the applications of which must be accompanied by the following documents:
- Exercise of subordinate professional activity (employment contract or promise of employment contract; statement issued by the Institute of Employment and Vocational Training / global
                                                                            contingent of employment opportunities; proof that you are entitled to practice the profession when it is regulated in Portugal);
- Practice of independent professional activity or entrepreneurial immigrants (partnership agreement or proposal or contract for the provision of services in the scope of liberal professions;
                                                                            statement issued by the competent entity in which it is qualified to perform the activity in National Territory (TN); who has undertaken or intends to carry out an investment
                                                                            transaction in TN of economic, social, scientific, technological or cultural relevance (proof that he has undertaken investment or has the financial means available in TN to do
                                                                            so);
- For teaching activity in a higher education establishment or highly qualified activity or culture (employment contract, service provision or research grant);
- For research, study, student exchange, work placement or volunteering (For Researchers: employment contract or hosting agreement with a research center or higher education institution;
                                                                                or have been admitted to a research center or higher education institution and have a research grant or grant; or present a term of responsibility signed by the research center
                                                                                or higher education institution that guarantees their admission, as well as the expenses for their stay .For study in Secondary Education: to be the minimum age and not to
                                                                                exceed the maximum age set by Ordinance No. 1079/07 of 10.12; student is enrolled, proof of reception by family, or proof of accommodation For unpaid internship: document
                                                                                issued by a recognized training company or organization; internship program; training contract; internship schedule. : document issued by an educational institution in which
                                                                                it was admitted or meets the conditions of admission Volunteering: document proving that it was admitted to a responsible organization, officially recognized in Portugal for
                                                                                a volunteer program);
In the context of higher education student mobility (for third-country nationals residing as higher education students in a Member State and applying to attend part of a study
                                                                                program already started in Portugal or to complete it with a study program proof of participation in a Community or bilateral exchange program or in which he / she has been
                                                                                admitted as a student in a Member State for a period of not less than 2 years; document issued by the higher education institution in which he / she has been admitted or meets
                                                                                the conditions of admission;
- For family reunification (the request for family reunification must be previously requested from the Foreigners and Borders Service - see the website of the (Foreigners and Borders
                                                                            Service);(notification of approval of family reunification delivered by SEF; proof of kinship invoked: birth or marriage certificate; minors or incapacitated: travel authorization
                                                                            for those exercising parental responsibility or guardianship)
- Retired, religious, income earners (religious: certificate of the church or community to which they belong, as long as they are recognized by the Portuguese legal order; retired :
                                                                            document proving the amount of the reform; people living on their own income: document proving income from movable or immovable property, or from intellectual property, or financial
                                                                            investments)
For Applicant only:
In favour of minors (only by parents or official guardians). If you have questions about the type of visa that best suits your situation, please contact the Consulate General of Portugal
                                                                        or the following website https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/ (https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/)
The issuing of national visas falls within the competence of the Portuguese authorities.
You are not required to give biometric data.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
 Long-stay visas entitle the holder to stay or establish residence in Portugal according to the duration of the stay and the reason for the stay.
Temporary stay visa
Allow stays for periods of 4 months with multiple entries. According to the reasons for the stay, there are 9 visa subtypes, the applications of which must be accompanied by the following
                                                                        documents:
- Medical treatment in officially or officially recognized health facilities (medical report; proof issued by recognized health facility officially that the applicant has secured hospitalization
                                                                            or outpatient treatment); in the case of patients sent under Cooperation Agreements, proof of medical appointment and appointment should be provided);
- Scope of transfers of workers between countries belonging to the World Trade Organization (WTO) for the provision of services or vocational training (supporting documents transferring
                                                                            the establishment of the same company or group of companies. NOTE: This visa applies to members or subordinate workers who have been employed for at least 1 year, who are senior
                                                                            management with decision-making powers, who are specific technicians essential to the activity of the company, who aim to receive vocational training);
- Temporary subordinate or independent activity (employment contract or promise of employment contract (6 months); statement by the Institute of Employment and Vocational Training (IEFP)
                                                                            that the promise or contract refers to the offer available to third-country nationals (subordinate work), or company contract or provision of services in the context of self-employment
                                                                            (self-employment);
- Exercise of scientific research activity, teaching activity in a Higher Education establishment and highly qualified activity (employment contract, service contract or research scholarship,
                                                                            less than one year);
- Practice of amateur sports activity (contract or promise of employment contract for less than one year; document of the respective federation confirming the exercise of the sport
                                                                            activity; term of responsibility signed by the association or sports club regarding accommodation, health expenses and expenses for the return to the country of origin);
- Remain in the National Territory for periods of more than 3 months in duly substantiated cases, namely the fulfilment of international commitments (proof of exceptional status; service
                                                                            contract; certificate of technical qualifications for service provision);
- Accompanying a family member subject to medical treatment (family relationship document; medical report of the family member who accompanies; proof issued by an officially or officially
                                                                            recognized health establishment that the applicant has secured hospitalization or outpatient treatment).
- Seasonal work for more than 90 days (contract or promise of employment contract with a temporary employment agency or employer established in Portugal that identifies the place, time
                                                                            and type of work, duration and remuneration and paid leave to which the worker is entitled; the duration of the contract will be a maximum of 9 months; proof of occupational accident
                                                                            insurance provided by the employer; certificate of possession of technical qualifications required for the provision of the service in question);
- Attendance to a course in a vocational education or training establishment (proof of housing conditions; proof of existence of means of subsistence; document issued by a vocational
                                                                            education or training institution confirming the applicant's admission to a course of less than 1 year ).
For Applicant only:
In favour of minors (only by parents or official guardians). If you have questions about the type of visa that best suits your situation, please contact the Consulate General of Portugal
                                                                        or the following website https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/ (https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/)
The issuing of national visas falls within the competence of the Portuguese authorities.
You are not required to give biometric data.

                                                                            education or training institution confirming the applicant's admission to a course of less than 1 year ).
For Applicant only:
In favour of minors (only by parents or official guardians). If you have questions about the type of visa that best suits your situation, please contact the Consulate General of Portugal
                                                                        or the following website https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/ (https://www.vistos.mne.pt/en/)
The issuing of national visas falls within the competence of the Portuguese authorities.
You are not required to give biometric data.

## Visa Fees
The visa fees applicable to the national visa application are as mention below table:

[Table:Temporary Stay Visa]
The applicants who wish to appeal their visa application have to pay a 329 MZN service fee and a 5553 (75 euros) visa fee.
Applicant who are exempt from Visa fees:
- Descendants of holders of Residence Permit under the provisions of family reunification.
- Foreign citizens who benefit from scholarships granted by the Portuguese State.
- To carry out highly qualified research activities (residence and temporary stay).
- Patients beneficiaries of Cooperation Agreements with Portugal in the field of health and
The fee is not refundable, refers only to the processing of the visa application and does not imply its issuance.
A service fee of 40 EUR (inclusive of 16% tax) will be added to the applicable visa fee for each application. This fee is payable only at the VFS Visa Application Center and must be paid in local currency, based on the current month’s exchange rate provided by the Consulate. Family members of EU/EEA or Swiss citizens are exempt from paying the service fee.
All the payments At Visa application centre are to be made in Cash,
We also offer an SMS Notification Service at a cost of 148 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a quick and convenient way to know the status of your visa application. This service is optional.
We also have to offer a Preferred VIP Lounge Service at a cost of 4442 MT (16 % tax included ). This service is a comfortable way to apply for your visa and offers faster service to its users for submission process only.
We also have to offer Photocopy service at the cost of 15 MT (16 % tax included ).
We also have to offer Photograph service at the cost of 370 MT (16 % tax included ).
We also have to offer form filling service at the cost of 370 MT (16 % tax included ).
After the application has been submitted to the VFS Visa Application Center, there is no refund of the Service fees.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/moz/en/prt/travel-insurance)
DO NOT ALLOW TWO NATIONALS (PORTUGUESE / MOZAMBICAN) OR NATIONALITY HOLDERS OF ONE OF THE SCHENGEN STATES,TO SUBMIT A NATIONAL VISA APPLICATION
TO APPLY THE VISA APPLICATION MUST BE MADE WITH AT LEAST 30 WORKING DAYS BEFORE THE PLAN DATE FOR THE TRAVEL: IN THIS CASE OF THE VISAS OF TEMPORARY STAY AND 60 DAYS FOR RESIDENCE VISA.
                                                                        THERE ARE EXCEPTIONS EXPECTED TO THESE TERMS IN ACCORDANCE WITH CURRENT LEGISLATION.
List of documents to be submitted by visa applicants in Maputo:
Documents:
- Valid travel document (+ 3 months after expected stay);
- 1 identical passport-type photographs, up-to-date and in good condition for the applicant's identification;
- Permit to return (except for residence permits for family reunification and research, teacher in higher education or highly qualified activity);
- Valid travel insurance, covering the necessary expenses for medical reasons, including urgent medical care and possible repatriation;
- Request for consultation of the Portuguese criminal record by the Foreigners and Borders Service (SEF);
- Certificate of criminal record from the country of origin or where the applicant has lived for more than one year;
- Proof of accommodation conditions;
- Proof of the existence of means of subsistence (Ordinance No. 1563/2007, of 11.12 and Ordinance No. 760/2009, of 16.07);
- For minors or incapacitated, authorization from those who exercise parental responsibility or guardianship.
NOTE: Children under 16 years of age are exempt from filing criminal record documents.
The documents indicated are the base documents, without prejudice to any additional documentation that may be requested by the consular post.

## Photo Specifications
Note: The photographs submitted must meet with these specifications.
Please provide one (1) recent photograph (not more than 3 months old) of yourself. Minors up until 11 years of age (inclusively) should provide one (1) recent photograph. The photographs
                                                                        should be in colour and:
- Taken against a light background (white or off-white) so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear share quality and with the face in focus.
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print).
- Full face, non-smiling [without sunglasses, a hat/cap or other head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of their religious belief or ethnic background].
- Please stick (not stapled) one photograph on the Visa Application Form. Minors up until 11 years of age (inclusively) should provide a second photograph separately along with the
                                                                            documentation during submission at the visa application centre.
Note : Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. Kindly download
                                                                        the Photo Specification file for more information. Also Kindly do not paste or staple photograph on the application form.
                                                                        

## Processing Time
According to the common Visa Code signed by the Schengen member states, the processing time for an admissible visa application must not exceed 15 days. This period may be extended up
                                                                        to a maximum of 60 days in cases where additional documentation or scrutiny is needed. A visa can have one, two or multiple entries depending if you need to travel outside the Schengen
                                                                        area and back again during your visit. 

                                                                        Nationals of these countries should submit the application with a minimum advance indicated - 15 days from
                                                                        the date of trip since they are subject to prior consultation of the central authorities.

                                                                        To get an update on the status of your application: 

                                                                        You may visit our website https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html (https://www.vfsglobal.com/Portugal/Mozambique/English/index.html)                                                                        and click on the link “Track your Application”. Please keep your date of birth and receipt number information handy while tracking your application status.

                                                                        Or Email us at: info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.pormoz@vfshelpline.com)
Or Contact our Helpline at: +258 846281607
